top_signals.title: Tanker resale premiums soar as owners chase faster delivery amid war
top_signals.summary: As competition intensifies to secure ships that can be put to work moving crude immediately, the benchmark for prices in the tanker market is shifting from “vessel age” to “delivery timing.” With traffic restrictions in the Strait of Hormuz continuing for more than two months due to the Middle East war, resales of ships under ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Singapore’s LSFO arbitrage arrivals from the West to fall in May, June: traders
top_signals.summary: Singapore’s low sulfur fuel oil arrivals from the Western markets are set to fall for the fourth consecutive month in May, amid largely unfavorable arbitrage opportunities due to steep backwardation, multiple Singapore-based traders told Platts, part of S&P Global Energy, during the week to May 22. The monthly decline in Western inflows is expected to ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Gas Shipping Markets Growing Stronger
top_signals.summary: LNG The LNG spot market firmed this week with the arb opening, especially in the Atlantic basin. On the BLNG1 Australia–Japan route, 174k cbm vessels moved up from $64,800 last Friday to $67,000 today. The BLNG2 US Gulf–Continent route firmed from $94,300 a week ago to $100,400 today with the arb open, TFDE vessels being ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: EU ETS and FuelEU Maritime: The double dividend of LNG compliance in 2026
top_signals.summary: How LNG creates compliance value under both EU maritime regulations in 2026 — and why most shipping companies are only capturing half of it. 2026 is the year EU maritime emissions compliance stops being theoretical. EU ETS hits 100% liability and adds methane to the bill. FuelEU Maritime’s first penalties land. Two regulations running in ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: The index that moves with the market: why contracted rates deliver
top_signals.summary: Contracted rate indexes like XSI-C capture freight rates at the moment of booking, giving a two-week head start over cargo-loaded indexes. Find out why that lead time makes all the difference for hedging and index-linked contracts. Indexes based on contracted rates are the best foundation for hedging and index-linked contracts, Fabio Brocca, Chief Product Officer at Xeneta, explains why. There ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: China secures over 90% of global new VLCC orders: report
top_signals.summary: Since the beginning of this year, China’s shipbuilding industry has been continuously landing new orders, securing more than 90 percent of global new orders for very large crude carrier (VLCC) and establishing a dominant position in the global market, according to a CCTV News report on Wednesday. In Dalian, a port city in Northeast China’s ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: I’m a Manchester United fan and marketing expert but Arsenal are cool
top_signals.summary: Arsenal’s long-awaited success on and off the field has been earned with smart, patient planning, says Pitch founder (and Man Utd supporter) Henry Chappell. As a Manchester United fan since before the Fergie years, Arsenal’s march to Premier League glory and a Champions League final is a tough watch. And it’s just as hard to [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Sunak calls for minimum wage quango to be abolished
top_signals.summary: Former Chancellor and Prime Minister Rishi Sunak has said that his decisions to hike the national minimum wage backfired as he called for a quango overseeing low pay to be abolished.  Sunak appeared to admit that his decision to increase the national living wage has contributed to job struggles faced by young people.  In an [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Maritime Safety Committee (MSC 111): Key outcomes on autonomous ships, GHG safety and situation in the Strait of Hormuz
top_signals.summary: The International Maritime Organization’s Maritime Safety Committee met for its 111th session from 13 to 22 May 2026 in London, United Kingdom. The meeting was chaired by Mr. Theofilos Mozas of Greece. Key outcomes are outlined below, with full meeting summary to follow. First global code for autonomous ships IMO adopted a new International Code ...

top_signals.title: Could LIV Golf really file for Chapter 11 bankruptcy?
top_signals.summary: The potential bankruptcy of LIV Golf could see players leverage their power to take over the isolated business, according to experts. The rebel golf league, featuring the likes of Bryson DeChambeau and Jon Rahm, has reportedly begun bankruptcy plans after its Saudi Arabian backers – the Public Investment Fund – confirmed that they’ll cease funding [...]

top_signals.title: Moeve strengthens operations with new tanker agreement
top_signals.summary: UK-based Suardiaz Group’s subsidiary, Suardiaz Energy, has signed an agreement to deploy its newly acquired chemical tanker, RS Onza, for Spanish marine fuel supplier Moeve. The IMO Type 2 bunker tanker is capable of transporting methanol and was recently delivered to Suardiaz in Singapore. The vessel will operate across European ports, with a primary focus ...
